symptoms.symptom_name: Situational inability to maintain an erection with the partner, lost at attempted intercourse

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Situation-specific failure is characteristic of a psychogenic mechanism.

symptoms.symptom_name: Preserved full rigid erections on waking and overnight

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Preserved nocturnal tumescence demonstrates intact vascular and neurological erectile capacity.
Rigid nocturnal erections are inconsistent with a significant vascular cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Preserved full erections during solo masturbation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal erections in a low-pressure solo context confirm the mechanism is contextual, not organic.
Organic causes impair erections in all contexts, not selectively.

symptoms.symptom_name: Onset tightly correlated with acute stress and work-related anxiety

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A clear psychological trigger temporally linked to onset supports a psychogenic cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute panic symptoms — palpitations, sweating, feeling wound up

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Autonomic panic features accompanying the episodes reflect the sympathetic overdrive driving the erectile failure.

symptoms.symptom_name: No medication known to impair erectile function

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of SSRIs, beta-blockers or other offending agents excludes a pharmacological cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: No cardiovascular risk factors or exertional chest symptoms

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of cardiovascular risk factors lowers the pre-test probability of a vasculogenic cause but does not remove the need for formal risk assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal serum morning total testosterone (if measured)

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Hypogonadism not formally excluded; unlikely given preserved spontaneous erections.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal morning testosterone excludes clinically significant hypogonadism — a laboratory result never available in real time via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal objective nocturnal penile tumescence test (if performed)

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Rarely needed; the history of preserved spontaneous erections already demonstrates intact capacity.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective preserved nocturnal tumescence confirms a psychogenic rather than organic cause — a specialist investigation, not available via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intact peripheral pulses and normal neurological examination of the lower limbs and perineum

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal vascular and neurological examination supports intact organic function — a clinician-measured sign the AI Doctor cannot obtain remotely.
Mildly elevated BP and tachycardia are consistent with the acute anxiety state; a sealed clinician measurement the AI Doctor infers only indirectly from reported palpitations.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Cardiovascular risk assessment (BP, lipids, fasting glucose/HbA1c)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: New-onset ED is a cardiovascular sentinel; risk assessment is good practice even when a psychogenic cause is likely.
Excludes hypogonadism as a contributing organic cause.
